title: The Interpretive Network of Authority
description: Investigation of the relationship between apostolic discourse and political reality.

clues.title: Opening to the Nations
clues.content: Abolition of the Law's walls and preaching of the 'unsearchable riches of Christ'

clues.title: The Church as Body
clues.content: Continuous journey through persecutions so the Church becomes Christ's Body throughout the oikoumene

clues.title: Rhetoric as Servant
clues.content: Chrysostom's golden tongue gives voice to Paul's epistles in the cities, turning theory into life

clues.title: Order and Authority
clues.content: Authority under God as means to ensure social cohesion without destroying society

clues.title: The Metaphor of the Ties
clues.content: Authority as instrument of God's goodness to avoid anarchy, like the ties (supports) of a house

clues.title: Pedagogical Fear
clues.content: Fear of rulers functions pedagogically to maintain spiritual vigilance in times of stability

clues.title: Kenosis and Humility
clues.content: Rejection of all human glory through Christ's mindset who humbled himself

clues.title: Resistance to Worldly Power
clues.content: Kenosis as model for the bishop's stance against imperial court claims

clues.title: True Authority
clues.content: True authority belongs to Christ; salvation is not in decrees but in participatory experience

clues.title: Insidious Persecution
clues.content: Persecution in Christian empire is more dangerous as it comes from those who bear Christ's name

clues.title: Political Expediency
clues.content: Church stability often becomes a trap for the state to legislate on matters of faith

clues.title: Prophetic Correction
clues.content: Chrysostom as 'New Paul' and 'John the Forerunner' corrects the moral decay of the court with parrhesia
